title: OpnForm
description: Beautiful open-source form builder to create and share forms, surveys and quizzes with no code, a self-hosted alternative to Typeform and Google Forms.
installation: Multi-container Laravel (PHP/API) + Nuxt (Vue/client) stack. THIS template deploys the PRIMARY API/server image jhumanj/opnform-api:latest (Laravel + nginx + php-fpm on port 80), which serves the REST API and runs DB migrations on boot. Rotate APP_KEY (base64:$(openssl rand -base64 32)), JWT_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32), and the Postgres password (default 'opnform') before exposing publicly. Companion services to deploy separately, ALL from the SAME jhumanj/opnform-api:latest image, sharing this template's env: (1) api-worker -- override command to run the queue worker `php artisan queue:work`; (2) api-scheduler -- run the cron scheduler `php artisan schedule:work`. Also deploy the FRONTEND as a separate app from jhumanj/opnform-client:latest (Nuxt SSR, listens on port 3000) with NUXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE pointing at this api's /api URL. The reference compose puts an nginx ingress in front that routes / to the client and /api,/forms,/storage to this api; replicate that routing at the Kubero ingress (path-based) or front both with a small nginx. Postgres (DB_CONNECTION=pgsql) and Valkey/Redis add-ons are bundled here. Set FRONTEND_URL to the public client URL after deploying it. Local file uploads/avatars/exports persist on the /usr/share/nginx/html/storage/app volume; for production object storage set FILESYSTEM_DISK=s3 and add S3-compatible storage env (no S3 add-on is provisioned by this template). Configure SMTP (MAIL_* env) for outbound email after install.
